drinks.name: Negroni
drinks.description: A perfect trinity of gin, sweet vermouth, and Campari — the Negroni is equal parts bitter revelation and sweet reward. Master this, and you understand the soul of Italian aperitivo culture.
drinks.garnish: Orange peel, expressed and placed on rim
drinks.tastingNotes: Bittersweet orange, herbal botanicals, light floral finish
recipe: Combine all three ingredients in a mixing glass filled with ice.
Stir for 25–30 seconds until well-chilled and slightly diluted.
Strain into a rocks glass over a single large ice cube.
Hold an orange peel over the glass and twist firmly to express the oils onto the surface.
Run the peel around the rim, then place it over the ice as garnish.

drinks.name: Dry Martini
drinks.description: Chilled to near freezing, the Dry Martini is an exercise in restraint — gin-forward, bone dry, impeccably stirred. The ratio is your signature. This is ours.
drinks.garnish: Lemon twist or a single castelvetrano olive
drinks.tastingNotes: Crisp, botanical, dry vermouth whisper, long clean finish
ingredients: 2.5 oz London Dry Gin (or premium vodka)
recipe: Place your coupe or martini glass in the freezer at least 20 minutes before serving.
Add gin (or vodka), dry vermouth, and bitters to a mixing glass filled with fresh ice.
Stir for 40 seconds — this is a stirred drink, never shaken.
Strain into the chilled glass.
Express a lemon twist over the surface and add as garnish, or drop in a single olive.

drinks.name: Old Fashioned
drinks.description: The original cocktail. A measured spoonful of sugar, a few dashes of bitters, and the finest bourbon you own. There is nothing to hide behind here — only the whiskey.
drinks.garnish: Expressed orange peel and a Luxardo cherry
drinks.tastingNotes: Rich vanilla oak, caramel sweetness, subtle citrus, warming spice
recipe: Add the syrup (or sugar cube) directly into your rocks glass.
Dash both bitters over the sugar.
If using a sugar cube, add a small splash of water and muddle until fully dissolved.
Add the whiskey and stir briefly to combine.
Add a large ice cube and continue stirring for 15 seconds to chill and dilute.
Express a wide orange peel over the glass and add a Luxardo cherry as garnish.

drinks.name: Boulevardier
drinks.description: Swap gin for bourbon and the Negroni becomes something altogether different — deeper, warmer, and unmistakably autumnal. A drink for slow evenings and long conversations.
drinks.tastingNotes: Caramel, bitter orange, dark cherry, warming whiskey spice
recipe: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with plenty of ice.
Stir for 30 seconds until properly chilled and diluted.
Strain into a chilled coupe or over a large ice cube in a rocks glass.
Express an orange peel over the surface and add as garnish.

drinks.name: Margarita
drinks.description: Made properly — with good blanco tequila, freshly squeezed lime, and real triple sec — the Margarita is transcendent. The difference between a great one and a mediocre one is entirely the ingredients.
drinks.tastingNotes: Bright citrus, agave sweetness, gentle saline, clean finish
recipe: Run a lime wedge around the outer rim of your glass and dip in coarse sea salt.
Combine tequila, Cointreau, and fresh lime juice in a shaker with ice.
Shake vigorously for 12–15 seconds.
Strain into the prepared glass — over fresh ice for rocks style, neat for coupe.
Garnish with a thin lime wheel on the rim.

drinks.name: Daiquiri
drinks.description: Three ingredients, infinite nuance. A well-made Daiquiri is one of the most elegant drinks a home bartender can master — and one of the hardest to make badly once you know the ratio.
drinks.garnish: Lime wheel or expressed lime twist
drinks.tastingNotes: Tart fresh lime, light cane sweetness, delicate rum florals, crisp and clean
ingredients: 0.75 oz Simple Syrup (or 0.5 oz for a drier pour)
recipe: Place a coupe glass in the freezer at least 15 minutes before serving.
Combine rum, fresh lime juice, and simple syrup in a shaker.
Add ice and shake hard for 12–14 seconds.
Double-strain through a fine mesh strainer into the chilled coupe to remove ice chips.
Garnish with a thin lime wheel floated on the surface.

drinks.name: Espresso Martini
drinks.tagline: When the night needs a second wind
drinks.description: Bold espresso, smooth vodka, and a touch of coffee liqueur — the Espresso Martini is unapologetically indulgent. The frothy crown, when achieved, is its own reward.
drinks.garnish: Three espresso beans, floated in the center of the foam
drinks.tastingNotes: Intense roasted coffee, vanilla, light bitterness, creamy persistent foam
ingredients: 0.75 oz Kahlúa or good coffee liqueur
recipe: Pull a fresh espresso shot and let it cool for 60–90 seconds — hot espresso kills the foam.
Combine vodka, coffee liqueur, espresso, and syrup in a shaker with ice.
Shake very vigorously for 15 seconds — the hard shake is what creates the foam.
Double-strain into a chilled coupe glass.
Float exactly three espresso beans in the center of the foam.

drinks.name: Casa Spritz
drinks.description: Our take on the beloved spritz — slightly more complex, a touch more bitter, with a long citrus and herbal finish. Best served at golden hour with nowhere to be.
drinks.garnish: Orange half-wheel and a fresh rosemary sprig
drinks.tastingNotes: Effervescent, bitter orange, elderflower, herbal rosemary, light stone fruit
recipe: Fill a large wine glass generously with fresh ice.
Add Aperol and elderflower liqueur directly over the ice.
Pour Prosecco slowly down the side of the glass to preserve the bubbles.
Top with a splash of sparkling water.
Gently stir once with a long spoon to barely combine.
Garnish with a half-wheel of orange and a sprig of fresh rosemary.
